adjudication.root_cause: The protocol separated terminal eligibility from remediation syntactically but did not define whether eligibility referred to the full requested terminal state, an allowed subset, or the availability of remediation. The model therefore marked six blocked full requests eligible because a safe remediation path existed.
adjudication.secondary_ambiguity: The action definitions did not state that narrow requires an immediately executable subset and rollback requires a prior admitted or effected state. That left stale-public-surface and never-applied incomplete-rollback cases ambiguous between narrow/defer and rollback/defer.
adjudication.why_not_rescore: V3 prospectively required exact field equality. Interpreting eligibility as remediation availability or broadening neighboring actions after seeing the output would change the construct retrospectively.

prospective_v4_requirements: Name the full requested terminal state in every task and define terminal eligibility only over that complete state now.
State that the existence of a safe remediation or executable subset does not make the full request eligible.
Define narrow as an immediately executable authorized subset and rollback as reversion of a prior admitted, mutated, or deployed state.
Preserve the eight-way residual vocabulary, which matched 8/8, and the remediation actions that matched 6/8.
Use a new protocol identity, one-shot local generation, candidate-before-label closure, two evaluators, 7/8 floors, zero disagreement, and no hosted-chat dependency.
non_claims: This diagnosis does not change or rescore v3.
Eight valid JSON records and exact residuals do not establish useful governance reasoning.
No campaign, support, publication, release, safety, rollback, or causal result follows.
